examples: I was overwhelmed by the amount of work.
Don't feel overwhelmed; take it step by step.
context: Oh yeah, way more. I was managing property. I was leasing. I was doing maintenance supervisor work Normally you have one person for each one of those jobs And I did it for three four years It took me from this happy positive person to oh my gosh, I'm stressed I got to do this. I got to do that. There's no time. You feel overwhelmed every day And then I just told myself I need one job and I wasn't able to find it there at that company So that's what prompted me to start checking out the job market It worked out good. It turned out a former boss of mine from years ago called me and offered me this job Nice. Oh, I went with it. Here we are again And I told my boss I only need this job for about four to five years And then hopefully I can retire and then life will slow down a bit I hope maybe not. I'm not interested in climbing corporate ladders anymore And I made that clear right up front When you were job hunting do you have any specific memories?

examples: I was sweaty after the workout.
His sweaty hands made it hard to grip the rope.
It's normal to feel sweaty in a sauna.
context: Our second guests and myself they called me and said We're very sorry we're having technical issues Can we reschedule for later today I said yeah that if you just want to shoot me a text And we'll do the interview I got tired of waiting so I went out and started cutting a tree down All of a sudden they called And I'm all sweaty at this point I've got t-shirt full of sawdust and all this stuff And so I didn't want to miss it though this is my one shot So I ran in the house, took my nasty sweaty sawdust filled t-shirt off Put on a nice dry shirt, go my hair set down Okay there I interviewed with two people from corporate They were asking me all kinds of questions about Why are you leaving your current job?
